feed.title: Google Cloud: BigQuery
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: BigQuery

entry.content: You can now use the visual graph modeler in BigQuery Studio to define BigQuery graph nodes and edges from your BigQuery tables and edit graph schema. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Studio now includes a visual graph modeler, allowing you to define and edit graph nodes and edges directly from your BigQuery tables. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New Visual Graph Modeler in BigQuery Studio

entry.content: Dataproc is now called Managed Service for Apache Spark. The names for associated API, client library, CLI, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) resources remain unchanged.
entry.summary: Dataproc has been renamed to Managed Service for Apache Spark. While the service name has changed, associated API, client library, CLI, and IAM resource names will remain the same.
entry.title: Announcement: Dataproc is now Managed Service for Apache Spark

entry.content: BigLake is now called Google Cloud Lakehouse. BigLake metastore is now called the Lakehouse runtime catalog. The names for associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) remain unchanged and still reference BigLake.
entry.summary: BigLake has been renamed to Google Cloud Lakehouse, with its metastore now called the Lakehouse runtime catalog. While the core product and metastore have new names, associated APIs, client libraries, CLI commands, and IAM resources will continue to reference BigLake.
entry.title: Announcement: BigLake Renamed to Google Cloud Lakehouse

entry.content: Dataplex Universal Catalog is now called Knowledge Catalog. The API, client library, CLI, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) names remain unchanged. For more information, see Knowledge Catalog overview.
entry.summary: Dataplex Universal Catalog has been renamed to Knowledge Catalog. While the name has changed, the API, client library, CLI, and IAM names remain the same. For more details, refer to the Knowledge Catalog overview.
entry.title: Announcement: Dataplex Universal Catalog Renamed to Knowledge Catalog

entry.content: Looker Studio is now called Data Studio. The website and endpoint change from lookerstudio.google.com to datastudio.google.com. You do not need to update your reports for this change, as Data Studio automatically redirects to the new domain. However, if your company uses proxies to restrict access to external sites, your IT administrator needs to add the new domain to your access control list (ACL). The names for associated API, client library, CLI, and Identity and Access Management (IAM) resources remain unchanged. For more information, see Data Studio returns as new home for Data Cloud assets.
entry.summary: Looker Studio has been renamed to Data Studio. The website and endpoint have been updated to datastudio.google.com, with automatic redirects in place for existing reports. IT administrators may need to update proxy access controls to include the new domain.
entry.title: Announcement: Data Studio Announcement

entry.content:
BigQuery graphs now support the following features:

  • You can query graphs using natural language in Conversational Analytics.
  • You can add descriptions and synonyms to the labels and properties in your graphs.
  • For some types of graphs you can define measures, which lock an aggregation to a key to help you perform complex aggregations without overcounting. To query measures, you transform your graph into a flattened table by using the GRAPH_EXPAND TVF, and then query measures in that table with the AGG function.

These features are in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery graphs now support querying with natural language, adding descriptions and synonyms to labels and properties, and defining measures for complex aggregations. These new capabilities are currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New BigQuery Graph Features

entry.content: You can now use the Data Engineering Agent to build, modify, and troubleshoot data pipelines in BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Build, modify, and troubleshoot data pipelines in BigQuery with the new Data Engineering Agent. This powerful tool is now generally available, simplifying your data engineering workflows.
entry.title: Feature: Data Engineering Agent for BigQuery Now Generally Available

entry.content: You can now visualize BigQuery graph query results and graph schemas directly in BigQuery Studio, without the need of a notebook environment. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Studio now allows direct visualization of BigQuery graph query results and schemas, eliminating the need for a separate notebook environment. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Visualize BigQuery Graph Results in Studio

entry.content: Starting July 25, 2026, the BigQuery Data Transfer Service for Facebook Ads connector will update the data type mapping for the ActionValue field in the AdInsightsActions report from INT to FLOAT.
entry.summary: The Facebook Ads connector will update the data type for the ActionValue field from INT to FLOAT starting July 25, 2026. This change affects the AdInsightsActions report. Ensure your data pipelines are prepared for this type modification.
entry.title: Change: Facebook Ads connector data type update

entry.content:
The following features have been added to Python UDFs during Preview:

  • Vectorized UDFs with Apache Arrow. You can now create vectorized Python UDFs using the Apache Arrow RecordBatch interface for improved performance.
  • Cloud Monitoring integration. Python UDFs now export metrics to Cloud Monitoring, including CPU utilization, memory utilization, and maximum concurrent requests per instance.
  • Container request concurrency. A new option, container_request_concurrency, is available for the CREATE FUNCTION statement. This option controls the maximum number of concurrent requests per Python UDF container instance.
  • New quotas. Python UDFs are now subject to new quotas on image storage bytes (10 GiB per project per region) and mutation rate (30 per minute per project per region).
  • Cost visibility. Python UDF costs can be seen in the external_service_costs column in the INFORMATION_SCHEMA.JOBS view and in the ExternalServiceCosts field in the Job API.
entry.summary: Enhancements to Python UDFs are now in Preview, including vectorized UDFs with Apache Arrow for better performance, Cloud Monitoring integration for metrics, and new options for container concurrency. Cost visibility and updated quotas are also included.
entry.title: Feature: New Python UDF Features Available

entry.content: You can now migrate metadata from external data catalogs to BigLake tables for Apache Iceberg. This feature supports external data catalogs such as such as Apache Hive Metastore and Apache Iceberg REST Catalog. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now migrate metadata from external data catalogs, including Apache Hive Metastore and Apache Iceberg REST Catalog, to BigLake tables for Apache Iceberg. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Metadata migration to BigLake tables

entry.content:
You can use the BigQuery MCP server to perform a range of data-related tasks with your AI applications including:

  • Examining BigQuery resources.
  • Generating accurate and efficient SQL queries.
  • Securely executing queries.
  • Interpreting query results.

This feature is Generally Available (GA).
entry.summary: The BigQuery MCP server is now Generally Available, enabling you to perform various data-related tasks with your AI applications, including examining resources, generating and executing SQL queries, and interpreting results.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery MCP Server Now Generally Available

entry.content: You can now publish a BigQuery Conversational Analytics agent in Gemini Enterprise. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now publish a conversational analytics agent in Gemini Enterprise. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New conversational analytics agent

entry.content: You can now use the notebook gallery in the BigQuery web UI as your central hub for discovering and using prebuilt notebook templates. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: The notebook gallery is now generally available in the BigQuery web UI. This feature serves as a central hub for discovering and using prebuilt notebook templates, making it easier to get started with BigQuery analysis.
entry.title: Feature: Notebook Gallery Now Available in BigQuery

entry.content: Using folders to organize and control access to single file code assets is generally available (GA). In addition, you can perform bulk move and delete operations, refresh folder contents, and view full breadcrumb paths based on resource permissions. For more information, see Create and manage folders.
entry.summary: Organize and control access to single file code assets using folders, now generally available. This feature also supports bulk operations, folder content refresh, and breadcrumb path viewing based on permissions.
entry.title: Feature: Code asset folders now generally available

entry.content:
Conversational analytics now supports querying Lakehouse tables that connect to the Apache Iceberg REST catalog or are federated to an external catalog. For more information, see Query BigLake data with natural language.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Conversational analytics now supports querying Lakehouse tables connected to Apache Iceberg REST catalogs or federated to external catalogs. This enhancement allows you to use natural language to query BigLake data. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Query Lakehouse tables with conversational analytics

entry.content:
You can now use Colab Data Apps to transform your data analyses from Colab notebooks into polished, interactive applications.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Transform your Colab analyses into interactive applications with the new Colab Data Apps feature. This preview functionality allows you to easily create polished, shareable apps directly from your notebooks. Start building your data applications today.
entry.title: Feature: Colab Data Apps Now Available

entry.content:
You can now use the AI.KEY_DRIVERS function to identify segments of data that cause statistically significant changes to a summable metric.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Discover which data segments significantly impact your key metrics with the new AI.KEY_DRIVERS function. This feature helps you pinpoint the drivers behind statistically significant changes in your summable metrics. It is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Identify Key Data Drivers

entry.content: BigQuery Apache Iceberg external tables now support Iceberg version 3, including binary deletion vectors. For more information, see Apache Iceberg external tables. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery now supports Apache Iceberg version 3, including binary deletion vectors. This new capability enhances the management and performance of external tables using the Iceberg format. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery adds Iceberg 3 support

entry.content: BigQuery agent analytics is now generally available (GA) in the Google Agent Developer Kit. BigQuery agent analytics is an open source solution that lets you capture, analyze, and visualize multimodal agent interaction data at scale.
entry.summary: BigQuery agent analytics is now generally available in the Google Agent Developer Kit. This open source solution allows you to capture, analyze, and visualize multimodal agent interaction data at scale.
entry.title: Feature: Agent analytics now available

entry.content: A known issue has been resolved where a materialized view refresh could expose could expose masked or filtered data from fine grained access control policies in error messages. No further action is needed.
entry.summary: A resolved issue could have exposed masked or filtered data during materialized view refreshes. No user action is required.
entry.title: Announcement: Materialized view refresh issue resolved

entry.content: You can now use EXPORT DATA statements to reverse ETL BigQuery data to AlloyDB. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now use EXPORT DATA statements to reverse ETL BigQuery data to AlloyDB. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Export BigQuery data to AlloyDB

entry.content: Support for the AI.AGG function preview has been temporarily disabled. We are working to restore this feature as soon as possible.
entry.summary: Support for the AI.AGG function has been temporarily disabled. We are working to restore this feature as soon as possible.
entry.title: Feature: AI.AGG function support disabled

entry.content:
To reduce LLM token consumption and query latency when processing large datasets, enable optimized mode using the following managed AI functions:

  • AI.IF
  • AI.CLASSIFY

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Reduce LLM token consumption and query latency for large datasets by enabling optimized mode with new managed AI functions like AI.IF and AI.CLASSIFY. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Optimize LLM performance with new AI functions

entry.content:
The following managed AI functions use Gemini to help you filter, join, rank, and classify your data:

  • AI.IF: Filter and join text and unstructured data (such as images, PDFs, audio, or video) based on a condition described in natural language.
  • AI.SCORE: Rate text and unstructured data (such as images, PDFs, audio, or video) to rank your data by quality, similarity, or other criteria.
  • AI.CLASSIFY: Classify text and unstructured data (such as images, PDFs, audio, or video) into user-defined categories.

These functions are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Introducing new managed AI functions that leverage Gemini to enhance your data analysis capabilities. These functions allow you to filter, score, and classify text and unstructured data using natural language prompts. They are now generally available for use.
entry.title: Feature: New AI functions for data analysis

entry.content:
You can use visualization cells to automatically generate a visualization of any DataFrame in your notebook. You can customize the columns, chart type, aggregations, colors, labels, and title.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now automatically generate visualizations of your DataFrames directly within your notebooks. Customize charts with various options like columns, chart types, aggregations, colors, labels, and titles. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Visualize your data with notebook cells

entry.content: SQL cells in BigQuery notebooks are now generally available (GA).
entry.summary: SQL cells in BigQuery notebooks are now generally available. This feature allows you to seamlessly write and execute SQL queries directly within your notebooks.
entry.title: Feature: SQL cells now generally available

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from Snowflake to BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports transferring data from Snowflake to BigQuery. This new feature is generally available, simplifying data migration and integration between the two platforms.
entry.title: Feature: Snowflake data transfer to BigQuery now available

entry.content: You can now use stateful operations in continuous queries, which let you perform complex analysis by retaining information across multiple rows or time intervals using JOINs and windowing aggregations. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Continuous queries now support stateful operations, enabling complex analysis through joins and windowing aggregations. This preview feature allows you to retain information across rows and time intervals for more sophisticated data processing.
entry.title: Feature: Stateful Operations for Continuous Queries Now Available

entry.content:
You can now use BigQuery Graph to model your data as a graph and perform analysis on a large scale.

  • Create a graph directly from tables that store entities and relationships between entities. You don't need to modify your existing workflows or replicate your data to use it in graph queries.
  • Use Graph Query Language (GQL) to find complex, hidden relationships between data points that would be challenging to find using SQL.
  • Visualize your graph schema and graph query results in a notebook.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Analyze your data as a graph and discover complex relationships using the new graph features. Create graphs directly from your tables and query them with GQL for deeper insights. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New graph analysis capabilities

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports incremental data transfers when transferring data from Microsoft SQL Server to BigQuery. This feature is supported in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports incremental data transfers from Microsoft SQL Server to BigQuery. This new feature, currently in Preview, allows for more efficient data synchronization.
entry.title: Feature: Incremental SQL Server Transfers Now Available

entry.content: You can now use the @@session_id system variable with SQL user-defined functions, table functions, and logical views. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use the @@session_id system variable with SQL user-defined functions, table functions, and logical views. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Use session ID in UDFs and views

entry.content:
The BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports incremental data transfers for the following data source connectors:

  • MySQL
  • Oracle
  • PostgreSQL
  • ServiceNow

These features are supported in Preview.
entry.summary: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports incremental data transfers for MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, and ServiceNow connectors. This new feature, currently in Preview, allows for more efficient data synchronization.
entry.title: Feature: Incremental data transfers now available

entry.content: You can now use the built-in text embedding model embeddinggemma-300m in the AI.EMBED and AI.SIMILARITY functions. This model uses your BigQuery slots to generate embeddings at scale. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now use the built-in text embedding model `embeddinggemma-300m` in the `AI.EMBED` and `AI.SIMILARITY` functions. This model uses your BigQuery slots to generate embeddings at scale. This feature is in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New text embedding model in BigQuery ML

entry.content: You can now use the AI.AGG function to semantically aggregate unstructured input data based on natural language instructions. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Introducing the AI.AGG function for semantic aggregation of unstructured data using natural language. This new feature, currently in Preview, allows for intuitive data summarization based on your instructions.
entry.title: Feature: New AI function for data aggregation

entry.content: You can now use a custom organization policy to allow or deny specific operations on these BigQuery resources: tables, data policies, and row access policies. This feature is in preview.
entry.summary: You can now use custom organization policies to control operations on BigQuery tables, data policies, and row access policies. This new feature is currently in preview and offers more granular control over your BigQuery resources.
entry.title: Feature: Custom BigQuery Organization Policies Now Available

entry.content: You can now use the CREATE CONNECTION, ALTER CONNECTION SET OPTIONS, and DROP CONNECTION data definition language (DDL) statements to manage Cloud resource connections with GoogleSQL. Additionally, you can now use the connection user type and PROJECT resource type with GRANT and REVOKE data control language (DCL) statements to manage connection and project access. These features are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use DDL statements like CREATE, ALTER, and DROP CONNECTION to manage Cloud resource connections with GoogleSQL. Additionally, new DCL statements allow granular control over connection and project access. These features are now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Manage Cloud Resource Connections with GoogleSQL

entry.content:
The BigQuery Migration Service supports SQL translations from Snowflake SQL to GoogleSQL. This feature is now generally available (GA).

With this change, the translation service supports a wider variety of Snowflake SQL and has improved support for several data types. Among other changes, the translation service maps Snowflake INTEGER and zero-scale NUMERIC types up to precision 38 to INT64 type in GoogleSQL for improved performance by default.
entry.summary: The BigQuery Migration Service's SQL translation from Snowflake SQL to GoogleSQL is now generally available. This enhancement offers broader support for Snowflake SQL and improved data type mapping, including optimizations for integer and numeric types, leading to better performance.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery Migration Service now supports Snowflake SQL translation

entry.content: You can set the column granularity when you create a search index, which stores additional column information in your search index to further optimize your search query performance. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now set column granularity when creating a search index. This stores additional column information to optimize search query performance. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Optimize search performance with column granularity

entry.content:
BigQuery ObjectRef values now support the following:

  • You can run ObjectRef functions with either direct access or delegated access.
  • The OBJ.MAKE_REF function automatically fetches the latest Cloud Storage metadata and populates this in the ref.details field.
  • The OBJ.GET_READ_URL function returns a STRUCT value with a read URL and status columns and renders image results in the Cloud console. Use this function when you don't require a write URL.

These features are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery's ObjectRef now supports direct and delegated access for ObjectRef functions. The OBJ.MAKE_REF function automatically fetches the latest Cloud Storage metadata, and OBJ.GET_READ_URL provides a read URL and status for displaying images in the console. These capabilities are now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Easier access to BigQuery object references

entry.content:
The following forecasting and anomaly detection functions and updates are generally available (GA):

  • The AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES function supports providing a custom context window that determines how many of the most recent data points should be used by the model.
  • The AI.FORECAST function supports specifying the latest timestamp value for forecasting.

  • The AI.EVALUATE function supports the following:

  • You can provide a custom context window that determines how many of the most recent data points should be used by the model.

  • The function outputs the mean absolute scaled error for the time series.
entry.summary: New and updated forecasting and anomaly detection functions are now generally available. These include enhancements to AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES and AI.FORECAST, as well as AI.EVALUATE which now supports custom context windows and outputs mean absolute scaled error.
entry.title: Feature: Forecasting and anomaly detection functions now generally available

entry.content: You can now create BigQuery non-incremental materialized views over Spanner data to improve query performance by periodically caching results. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery now supports creating non-incremental materialized views over Spanner data. This feature, which is now generally available, helps improve query performance by periodically caching results.
entry.title: Feature: Materialized Views for Spanner Data Now Available

entry.content: You can now use Cloud resource connections with EXPORT DATA statements to reverse ETL BigQuery data to Spanner. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use Cloud resource connections with `EXPORT DATA` statements to reverse ETL BigQuery data to Spanner. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Export BigQuery data to Spanner

entry.content: The Gemini for Google Cloud API (cloudaicompanion.googleapis.com) is now enabled for existing BigQuery projects in the European jurisdiction.
entry.summary: The Gemini for Google Cloud API is now enabled for existing BigQuery projects in the European jurisdiction. This expansion provides European users with access to advanced AI capabilities within their BigQuery environment.
entry.title: Announcement: Gemini API now available in Europe

entry.content:
You can now use the BigQuery Migration Service MCP server to perform SQL translation tasks, including translating SQL queries into GoogleSQL syntax, generating DDL statements from SQL input queries, and getting explanations of SQL translations.

This feature is in preview.
entry.summary: The BigQuery Migration Service now supports SQL translation tasks, including converting SQL to GoogleSQL, generating DDL, and explaining translations. This preview feature enhances your migration workflow.
entry.title: Feature: SQL Translation with BigQuery Migration Service

entry.content: In BigQuery Data Transfer Service, you can monitor resource-level status reporting for Hive managed tables to track progress and view granular error details for individual tables. This feature is in preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now allows you to monitor resource-level status for Hive managed tables, providing detailed progress tracking and error insights for individual tables during transfers. This feature is currently in preview.
entry.title: Feature: Monitor Hive Table Transfer Status

entry.content: You can use the BigQuery migration assessment for Snowflake to assess the complexity of migrating from Snowflake to BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: A new tool is available to help assess the complexity of migrating from Snowflake to BigQuery. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Assess Snowflake to BigQuery Migration Complexity

entry.content: You can now use the BigQuery Data Transfer Service remote MCP server to enable AI agents to create, manage, and run data transfers. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now use the BigQuery Data Transfer Service remote MCP server to enable AI agents to create, manage, and run data transfers. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Manage BigQuery Data Transfers with AI Agents

entry.content:
The following functions are now generally available (GA):

  • AI.EMBED: create embeddings from text or image data.
  • AI.SIMILARITY: compute the semantic similarity between pairs of text, pairs of images, or across text and images.
entry.summary: BigQuery ML introduces two new generally available functions: AI.EMBED for creating embeddings from text or image data, and AI.SIMILARITY for computing semantic similarity between text and image pairs.
entry.title: Feature: New AI functions now available

entry.content: You can clean, transform, and enrich data from files in Cloud Storage and Google Drive in your BigQuery data preparations. For more information, see Prepare data with Gemini. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now clean, transform, and enrich data from Cloud Storage and Google Drive directly within BigQuery data preparations. This new feature is generally available and simplifies your data manipulation workflows.
entry.title: Feature: Data preparation with Gemini

entry.content: You can now use a custom organization policy to allow or deny specific operations on routines. This feature is in preview.
entry.summary: You can now use custom organization policies to allow or deny specific operations on routines. This feature is currently in preview.
entry.title: Feature: Control operations on routines

entry.content:
In BigQuery ML, you can now automatically deploy open models to Vertex AI endpoints. Automatically deployed models offer the following benefits:

  • Automatic Vertex AI resource management
  • Reserve open model resources by using Compute Engine reservations
  • Automatic or immediate open model undeployment to save costs

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery ML now automatically deploys open models to Vertex AI endpoints, simplifying resource management and reducing costs. This new feature offers automatic Vertex AI resource management, Compute Engine reservation options, and streamlined model undeployment for cost savings. It is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery ML now supports automatic deployment of open models to Vertex AI

entry.content:
BigQuery now lets you configure a global default location. This setting is used if the location isn't set or can't be inferred from the request. You can set the default location at the organization or project level.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now configure a global default location for BigQuery. This setting applies when no location is specified in a request and can be set at the organization or project level. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery default location setting

entry.content: BigQuery advanced runtime is now enabled as the default runtime for all projects.
entry.summary: The advanced runtime is now the default for all BigQuery projects. This change enhances performance and capabilities for your data processing tasks. No action is required from your end, as the update has been applied automatically.
entry.title: Change: BigQuery Default Runtime Updated

entry.content: You can now understand and debug BigQuery query performance with a visual mapping of your SQL query in the query execution graph. A heatmap highlights the steps that consume more slot-time. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery query performance debugging is now easier with a visual mapping of your SQL query in the query execution graph. This generally available feature highlights time-consuming steps with a heatmap, helping you identify and resolve performance bottlenecks.
entry.title: Feature: Debug BigQuery Performance with Visual Query Mapping

entry.content:
Updates to conversational analytics include the following improvements:

  • ObjectRef support: BigQuery conversational analytics now integrates with Google Cloud Storage through ObjectRef functions. This lets you reference and interact with unstructured data such as images and PDFs in Cloud Storage buckets in your conversational analysis.
  • BQML support: BigQuery conversational analytics now supports a set of BigQuery ML functions, including AI.FORECAST, AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES, and AI.GENERATE. These functions let you perform advanced analytics tasks with simple conversational prompts.
  • Chat with BigQuery results: You can now start conversations and chat with query results in BigQuery Studio (SQL editor).
  • Enhanced support for partitioned tables: BigQuery conversational analytics can now use BigQuery table partitioning. The agent can optimize SQL queries by using partitioned columns such as date ranges on a date-partitioned table. This can improve query performance and reduce costs.
  • Labels for agent-generated queries: BigQuery jobs initiated by the conversational analytics agent are now labeled in BigQuery Job History in the Google Cloud Console. You can identify, filter, and analyze the jobs run by the conversational analytics agent by referencing labels similar to {'ca-bq-job': 'true'}. These labels can help with the following tasks:
  • Monitor and attribute cost.
  • Audit agent activity.
  • Analyze agent-generated query performance.
  • Suggest next questions (clickable): When working with BigQuery conversational analytics, the agent now suggests questions that are directly clickable in the Google Cloud console.

This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery conversational analytics has been updated with ObjectRef and BQML support, enabling interaction with unstructured data and advanced AI functions. You can now chat with query results, benefit from enhanced partitioned table support for better performance and cost savings, and utilize new features like labeled queries and clickable suggested questions. This preview feature improves the usability and power of conversational analysis in BigQuery.
entry.title: Feature: Conversational Analytics Enhancements

entry.content:
You can create a remote model based on the Vertex AI gemini-embedding-001 model, or a remote model based on an open embedding model from Vertex Model Garden or Hugging Face that is deployed to Vertex AI.

You can then use the AI.GENERATE_EMBEDDING function with these remote models to generate embeddings. You can also use the AI.EMBED function directly with the gemini-embedding-001 model endpoint.

These features are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now create remote models in BigQuery ML based on Vertex AI embedding models, including gemini-embedding-001. Use the AI.GENERATE_EMBEDDING or AI.EMBED functions to generate embeddings directly within BigQuery. These features are now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Generate embeddings with BigQuery ML

entry.content:
You can now use the Pipelines & Connections page to streamline your data integration tasks by using guided, BigQuery-specific configuration workflows for services like BigQuery Data Transfer Service, Datastream, and Pub/Sub.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: The new Pipelines & Connections page offers guided, BigQuery-specific configuration workflows for services like BigQuery Data Transfer Service, Datastream, and Pub/Sub. This feature streamlines your data integration tasks. It is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Simplify data integration with new configuration workflows

entry.content: An updated version of the Simba ODBC driver for BigQuery is now available.
entry.summary: An updated version of the Simba ODBC driver for BigQuery has been released. This update provides the latest improvements and features for connecting to BigQuery data.
entry.title: Change: New Simba ODBC driver for BigQuery released

entry.content: You can now use an alternate syntax when you call the VECTOR_SEARCH function to improve query performance when you search for a single vector. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: A new alternate syntax for the VECTOR_SEARCH function is now available in preview. This change can improve query performance when searching for a single vector.
entry.title: Feature: Vector Search Alternate Syntax

entry.content: You can now use continuous queries to stream BigQuery data to Spanner in real time. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use continuous queries to stream BigQuery data to Spanner in real time. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery data streams to Spanner

entry.content: Monitor dataset replication latency and network egress bytes in Cloud Monitoring for BigQuery cross-region replication and managed disaster recovery. These metrics are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now monitor dataset replication latency and network egress bytes in Cloud Monitoring for BigQuery cross-region replication and managed disaster recovery. These new metrics are now generally available, providing enhanced visibility into your data replication and disaster recovery setups.
entry.title: Feature: Monitor BigQuery Replication and DR

entry.content: Effective June 1, 2026, BigQuery will limit legacy SQL use. This depends on whether your organization or project uses it from November 1, 2025, to June 1, 2026. If you don't use legacy SQL during this time, you won't be able to use it after June 1, 2026. If you do use it, your existing workloads will keep running, but new ones might not. For more information, see Legacy SQL feature availability.
entry.summary: Starting June 1, 2026, BigQuery will limit legacy SQL usage. If your organization or project does not use legacy SQL between November 1, 2025, and June 1, 2026, you will lose access. Existing workloads will continue to run, but new ones may be blocked.
entry.title: Change: BigQuery legacy SQL changes

entry.content:
You can now create and review custom glossary terms in BigQuery for a conversational analytics agent and you can review business glossary terms imported from Dataplex Universal Catalog for an agent. These terms help an agent interpret your prompts.

This feature is now in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now create and review custom glossary terms in BigQuery to help your conversational analytics agent better interpret your prompts. Additionally, review business glossary terms imported from Dataplex Universal Catalog. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Enhance your agent with custom glossary terms

entry.content: You can now undelete a dataset that is within your time travel window to recover it to the state that it was in when it was deleted. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now undelete a dataset within your time travel window to recover it to its state at the time of deletion. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Undelete BigQuery datasets

entry.content: You can now run global queries, which let you reference data stored in more than one region in a single query. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now run global queries in BigQuery, allowing you to reference data stored in multiple regions within a single query. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Run global queries in BigQuery

entry.content: After March 17, 2026, when you enable BigQuery, the BigQuery MCP server is automatically enabled.
entry.summary: Starting March 17, 2026, the BigQuery MCP server will be automatically enabled when you enable BigQuery. This change ensures that the necessary components are set up from the start for enhanced BigQuery functionality.
entry.title: Change: BigQuery MCP server enabled by default

entry.content: Control of MCP use with organization policies is deprecated. After March 17, 2026, organization policies that use the gcp.managed.allowedMCPServices constraint won't work, and you can control MCP use with IAM deny policies. For more information about controlling MCP use, see Control MCP use with IAM deny policies.
entry.summary: Control of MCP use with organization policies is deprecated and will stop working after March 17, 2026. You can now control MCP use with IAM deny policies. For more information, refer to the documentation on controlling MCP use with IAM deny policies.
entry.title: Deprecated: MCP Use Control: Deprecation of Organization Policies

entry.content: The AI.CLASSIFY function now supports classifying your input into multiple categories. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: The AI.CLASSIFY function in BigQuery ML now supports classifying input into multiple categories. This new multi-label classification capability is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: AI.CLASSIFY now supports multi-label classification

entry.content: You can now provide descriptions for the fields in your custom output schema when you use the AI.GENERATE and AI.GENERATE_TABLE functions. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now add descriptions to the fields in your custom output schema when using the AI.GENERATE and AI.GENERATE_TABLE functions. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Add descriptions to custom output schema fields

entry.content: You can now use dataset insights to understand relationships between tables in a dataset by generating relationship graphs and cross-table queries. You can automatically generate dataset summaries, infer relationships across tables, and receive suggestions for analytical questions. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Understand relationships within your datasets with the new Dataset Insights feature. Automatically generate relationship graphs, cross-table queries, and get suggestions for analytical questions. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New Dataset Insights Feature Released

entry.content: You can now run pipelines with three distinct execution methods: running all tasks, running selected tasks, and running tasks with selected tags. For more information, see Run a pipeline. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now run pipelines using three distinct execution methods: all tasks, selected tasks, or tasks with specific tags. This new feature offers greater flexibility in managing your pipeline runs and is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: New Pipeline Execution Options

entry.content:
You can now customize the scope of data documentation scans for BigQuery tables to generate specific insights. You can choose to generate only SQL queries, only table and column descriptions, or all insights.

You can also create one-time data scans that execute immediately upon creation, removing the need for a separate run command. These scans support a Time to Live (TTL) setting to automatically delete the scan resource after completion.

For more information, see Generate insights for a BigQuery table.
entry.summary: You can now customize data documentation scans for BigQuery tables to generate specific insights, choosing between SQL queries, table/column descriptions, or all insights. Additionally, create one-time scans that run immediately and have an optional Time to Live setting for automatic deletion.
entry.title: Feature: Customize data documentation scans

entry.content: Data transfers from the YouTube Channel and YouTube Content Owner data sources now support reach reports. For more information, see YouTube Channel report transformation and YouTube Content Owner report transformation.
entry.summary: Reach reports are now supported for data transfers from YouTube Channel and YouTube Content Owner. This update provides more detailed insights into your YouTube data. For more information, refer to the updated documentation on report transformations.
entry.title: Change: YouTube data transfer enhancements

entry.content: You can now associate data policies directly on columns. This feature enables direct database administration for controlling access and applying masking and transformation rules at the column level. This feature is now generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now associate data policies directly on columns in BigQuery. This feature, now generally available, enables database administrators to control access and apply masking and transformation rules at the column level.
entry.title: Feature: Column-level data policies now available

entry.content: Gemini in BigQuery now processes data in the same jurisdiction (US or EU) as your BigQuery datasets, or based upon user-specified location settings. For more information, see Where Gemini BigQuery processes your data.
entry.summary: Gemini in BigQuery now respects data residency, processing data within your BigQuery dataset's jurisdiction (US or EU) or according to your location settings. This ensures your data stays where you expect it to be.
entry.title: Announcement: Gemini BigQuery Data Residency

entry.content:
You can now pass parameterized queries from the BigQuery query editor in the Google Cloud console.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use parameterized queries directly from the BigQuery query editor in the Google Cloud console. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Parameterized queries in BigQuery editor

entry.content:
BigQuery now supports a RANDOM_HASH predefined masking rule. This rule returns a hash of the column's value using a salted hash algorithm, and it provides stronger security than the standard Hash (SHA-256) rule.

For more information, see Data masking rules.
entry.summary: BigQuery has introduced a new predefined masking rule, RANDOM_HASH. This rule offers enhanced security by hashing column values with a salted algorithm, providing a more robust alternative to the standard SHA-256 hash.
entry.title: Feature: New data masking rule available in BigQuery

entry.content: BigQuery now offers conversational analytics, which accelerates data analysis by enabling insights through natural language. Users can view a predefined sample agent, chat with their BigQuery data or custom agents, and access those agents even outside of BigQuery. They can also use supported BigQuery ML functions in verified queries and in chat. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Analyze your BigQuery data faster with conversational analytics. Ask questions in natural language to gain insights, and use supported BigQuery ML functions in chat. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Conversational Analytics for BigQuery

entry.content:
You can now create BigQuery ML models by using the Google Cloud console.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now create BigQuery ML models directly within the Google Cloud console. This feature is now generally available, simplifying the process of building machine learning models with BigQuery.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery ML models now available in the console

entry.content: The BigQuery change data capture feature has been renamed to BigQuery change data capture ingestion.
entry.summary: The BigQuery change data capture feature has been renamed to BigQuery change data capture ingestion. This change aims to clarify the functionality and improve discoverability of this feature within BigQuery.
entry.title: Change: BigQuery CDC Renamed

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from Shopify to BigQuery. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now transfer data from Shopify to BigQuery using the BigQuery Data Transfer Service. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Shopify data transfer to BigQuery available

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from Mailchimp to BigQuery. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now transfer data from Mailchimp to BigQuery using the BigQuery Data Transfer Service. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Mailchimp data transfer to BigQuery

entry.content: An updated version of the Simba JDBC driver for BigQuery is now available.
entry.summary: An updated version of the Simba JDBC driver for BigQuery has been released. This new version offers improvements and bug fixes for enhanced performance and stability.
entry.title: Change: New Simba JDBC driver available

entry.content: You can now use Gemini Cloud Assist to discover resources across your projects. For example, you can ask about a specific table's schema, or which tables contain demographic information about new users. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Gemini Cloud Assist now allows you to discover resources across your projects. You can ask questions about table schemas or specific data types. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Discover resources with Gemini Cloud Assist

entry.content: You can now optionally specify which model to use by passing an endpoint argument to the AI.IF, AI.SCORE, and AI.CLASSIFY functions.
entry.summary: You can now specify the exact model you want to use with the AI.IF, AI.SCORE, and AI.CLASSIFY functions by passing an endpoint argument. This provides more control over your AI model selections.
entry.title: Change: Choose your AI model

entry.content: You can now run queries that use the AI.IF, AI.SCORE, and AI.CLASSIFY functions by using your end-user credentials instead of a BigQuery connection.
entry.summary: You can now run queries using AI.IF, AI.SCORE, and AI.CLASSIFY functions with your end-user credentials. This change offers more flexibility in how you interact with these powerful AI capabilities.
entry.title: Change: New options for AI functions in BigQuery

entry.content: Support for table parameters in table-valued functions is restored.
entry.summary: Support for table parameters in table-valued functions has been restored. This fix ensures that table parameters can be correctly used within table functions.
entry.title: Fixed: Table function table parameters restored

entry.content: You can now use Gemini Cloud Assist to get information about your job history, such as why a particular query was slow or which queries were the most resource-intensive in the past day. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Gemini Cloud Assist now helps you understand your job history. You can analyze past queries to identify performance bottlenecks and resource usage. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Gemini Cloud Assist: Job History Insights

entry.content: BigQuery is now available in the Bangkok (asia-southeast3) region.
entry.summary: BigQuery is now available in the Bangkok region (asia-southeast3). This expansion brings BigQuery's powerful data warehousing capabilities closer to customers in Southeast Asia.
entry.title: Change: BigQuery now in Bangkok

entry.content: Dataform workflows, BigQuery notebooks, pipelines, and data preparations are enforcing strict act-as mode at the project level. To avoid failures and maintain automatic releases, you must use custom service accounts instead of the default Dataform service agent across all repositories. You must also grant the Service Account User role (roles/iam.serviceAccountUser) to the default Dataform service agent and relevant principals. For more information and to verify act-as permissions, see Use strict act-as mode.
entry.summary: Strict act-as mode is now enforced for Dataform workflows, BigQuery notebooks, pipelines, and data preparations at the project level. To prevent failures, update your repositories to use custom service accounts instead of the default Dataform service agent and grant the Service Account User role to the Dataform service agent and other relevant principals.
entry.title: Breaking: Action Required: Update Dataform Service Accounts

entry.content: You can now use the Google-developed, open source Java Database Connectivity (JDBC) driver for BigQuery to connect your Java applications to BigQuery. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Connect your Java applications to BigQuery using the new open-source JDBC driver. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery JDBC driver for Java now available

entry.content:
The CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE and LOAD DATA statements now support the following options:

  • time_zone: specify a time zone to use when loading data
  • date_format, datetime_format, time_format, and timestamp_format: define how date and time values are formatted in your source files
  • null_markers: define the strings that represent NULL values in CSV files.
  • source_column_match: specify how loaded columns are matched to the schema. You can match columns by position or by name.

These features are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery's CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE and LOAD DATA statements now support advanced options for handling time zones, date and time formats, null markers, and column matching. These features are now generally available, offering more flexibility and control over your data loading processes.
entry.title: Feature: New data loading options for BigQuery

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from PostgreSQL to BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now transfer data from PostgreSQL to BigQuery. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Transfer PostgreSQL data to BigQuery

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from Microsoft SQL Server to BigQuery. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports transferring data from Microsoft SQL Server to BigQuery. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: SQL Server data transfer to BigQuery available

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from MySQL to BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now transfer data from MySQL to BigQuery. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: MySQL data transfer to BigQuery now available

entry.content: You can now use the BigQuery Data Transfer Service to transfer data from blob storage sources, such as Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3), Azure Blob Storage, and Cloud Storage, into BigLake Iceberg tables in BigQuery. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports transferring data from blob storage sources like Amazon S3 and Azure Blob Storage into BigLake Iceberg tables. This new feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Data Transfer Service for Iceberg Tables

entry.content:
The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from the following data sources to BigQuery:

  • Klaviyo
  • HubSpot

These features are in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports data transfers from Klaviyo and HubSpot. These new features are currently in Preview. This allows you to easily move your marketing data into BigQuery for analysis.
entry.title: Feature: New Data Sources Available in BigQuery Transfer Service

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from Oracle to BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports transferring data from Oracle to BigQuery. This feature is now generally available, allowing for seamless integration.
entry.title: Feature: Oracle data transfer to BigQuery

entry.content:
You can now use the BigQuery remote MCP server to enable LLM agents to perform a range of data-related tasks.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery remote MCP server is now available in preview, allowing LLM agents to perform various data-related tasks. This new feature enables more advanced data analysis and manipulation directly through AI agents.
entry.title: Feature: LLM agents can now query BigQuery data

entry.content:
You can now enable autonomous embedding generation on tables that you make with the CREATE TABLE statement. When you do this, BigQuery maintains a column of embeddings on the table based on a source column. When you add or modify data in the source column, BigQuery automatically generates or updates the embedding column for that data.

You can also use the AI.SEARCH function, enabling semantic search on tables that have autonomous embedding generation enabled.

These features are in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery now supports autonomous embedding generation for tables created with the `CREATE TABLE` statement. This feature automatically maintains an embeddings column based on a source column, updating embeddings as data changes. Additionally, the `AI.SEARCH` function enables semantic search on tables with this feature enabled. These capabilities are currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Autonomous Embedding Generation

entry.content: An updated version of the ODBC driver for BigQuery is now available.
entry.summary: A new version of the ODBC driver for BigQuery has been released. This update includes the latest improvements and bug fixes for enhanced performance and stability when connecting to BigQuery.
entry.title: Change: Updated BigQuery ODBC driver released

entry.content: Search results in the Explorer pane in BigQuery Studio now show results in the current organization. You can use a drop-down menu to switch between organizations. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Search results in BigQuery Studio now show results in the current organization. You can use a drop-down menu to switch between organizations.
entry.title: Feature: Search results now in current organization

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports incremental data transfers when transferring data from Salesforce to BigQuery. This feature is supported in Preview.
entry.summary: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports incremental data transfers from Salesforce to BigQuery. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Incremental data transfers from Salesforce to BigQuery now in Preview

entry.content: An updated version of the JDBC driver for BigQuery is now available.
entry.summary: An updated version of the JDBC driver for BigQuery has been released. This new version includes improvements and bug fixes for a better user experience.
entry.title: Change: New BigQuery JDBC Driver Available

entry.content: You can set the default project and dataset for your pipeline in the SQLX options section, which simplifies task configuration by using these defaults for all tasks. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Set default project and dataset for your pipeline in SQLX options to simplify task configuration. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Default Project and Dataset for Pipelines

entry.content: You can now use the BigQuery Agent Analytics plugin within the Agent Development Kit to export agent interaction data directly into BigQuery. This plugin captures comprehensive logs of your agent's prompts, tool usage, and responses, enabling you to analyze and visualize agent performance metrics. The plugin leverages the BigQuery Storage Write API for efficient high-throughput streaming. For more information on how to leverage this plugin in your agent, see the Announcing BigQuery Agent Analytics for the Google ADK.
entry.summary: Export agent interaction data to BigQuery using the new Agent Analytics plugin in the Agent Development Kit. Analyze performance with comprehensive logs and efficient data streaming.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery Agent Analytics Plugin

entry.content: You can now use Gemini in BigQuery to fix and explain errors in your SQL queries. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now use Gemini in BigQuery to fix and explain errors in your SQL queries. This feature is in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Gemini in BigQuery Now Available

entry.content: You can use the JSON_FLATTEN function to extract all non-array values that are either directly in the input JSON value or children of one or more consecutively nested arrays in the input JSON value. This function is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Extract non-array values from JSON with nested arrays.
entry.title: Feature: New JSON_FLATTEN function

entry.content: Dataform now lets you automate the creation of BigLake tables for Apache Iceberg in BigQuery. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: Dataform now allows you to automate the creation of BigLake tables for Apache Iceberg in BigQuery. This new feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Dataform now supports Apache Iceberg tables in BigQuery

entry.content:
BigQuery ML now supports the following generative AI functions:

  • AI.GENERATE: generate free text to accomplish a wide range of tasks, such as translation, summarization, and classification, on any unstructured data, including images, audio, video, and documents. It can also perform entity extraction and generate structured output. This function is generally available (GA).
  • AI.EMBED: turn text, image, audio, video, or documents into embeddings. This function is in Preview.
  • AI.SIMILARITY: compute the semantic similarity between pairs of text, pairs of images, or across text and images. This function is in Preview.
  • You can use the AI.GENERATE_BOOL, AI.GENERATE_DOUBLE, and AI.GENERATE_INT functions to generate scalar values, which are convenient for filtering, scoring, and counting purposes.
  • Each of these functions supports authentication with end-user credentials (EUC) to set up the necessary Vertex AI permissions.

BigQuery ML now supports the following table-valued generative AI functions:

  • AI.GENERATE_TABLE: generate a table of structured output from unstructured data including text, images, audio, and video.
  • AI.GENERATE_TEXT is the new, preferred version of ML.GENERATE_TEXT, which has the same functionality but with simplified column output names.
  • AI.GENERATE_EMBEDDING is the new, preferred version of ML.GENERATE_EMBEDDING, which has the same functionality but with simplified column output names.
  • These functions are all generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery ML now offers a suite of powerful generative AI functions, including AI.GENERATE for text and structured output, AI.EMBED for creating embeddings from various data types, and AI.SIMILARITY for semantic comparisons. These enhancements allow for advanced natural language processing and data analysis directly within BigQuery, with support for end-user credentials for secure access. The update also introduces table-valued functions like AI.GENERATE_TABLE and preferred versions of text and embedding generation functions.
entry.title: Feature: New Generative AI Functions in BigQuery ML

entry.content:
You can now use Gemini 3.0 when you call generative AI functions in BigQuery, such as AI.GENERATE. You must use the full global endpoint argument:

https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/global/publishers/google/models/gemini-3-pro-preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery now supports Gemini 3.0 for generative AI functions. Use the full global endpoint for integration.
entry.title: Feature: Gemini 3.0 in BigQuery

entry.content: You can now publish data insights, including query recommendations and auto-generated table and column descriptions, to the Dataplex Universal Catalog. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now publish data insights, including query recommendations and auto-generated table and column descriptions, to the Dataplex Universal Catalog. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Data Insights Now Available in Dataplex Universal Catalog

entry.content: In the query execution graph, you can now use the query text heatmap to identify which query text contributes to stages that consume more slot time, and to see query plan details for those stages. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Identify query text contributing to slot time consumption using the new query text heatmap in the query execution graph. This feature, currently in Preview, allows you to view query plan details for time-consuming stages.
entry.title: Feature: Query text heatmap for query execution graph

entry.content: You can use folders to organize and control access to single file code assets, such as notebooks, saved queries, data canvases, and data preparation files. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Use folders to organize and control access to single file code assets like notebooks, saved queries, and more. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Organize code assets with folders

entry.content: You can now share SQL stored procedures in BigQuery sharing listings and enable role-based authorization for stored procedures. These features are in preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery now allows sharing SQL stored procedures and managing role-based authorization for them. These features are currently in preview.
entry.title: Feature: Share SQL stored procedures in BigQuery

entry.content: The BigQuery Overview page is now your hub for discovering tutorials, features, and resources to help you get the most out of BigQuery. It provides guided paths for users of all skill levels. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: The BigQuery Overview page is a new hub for tutorials, features, and resources, offering guided paths for all users. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery Overview Page Now Available

entry.content:
You can now use the interactive SQL translator, the translation API, and the batch SQL translator to translate the following SQL dialects into GoogleSQL:

  • Apache Impala SQL
  • GoogleSQL (BigQuery)

Impala SQL translation can be used to migrate Cloudera and Apache Hadoop SQL workloads that use Impala as a query engine.

GoogleSQL (BigQuery) translation can be used to verify and iteratively customize your translated SQL queries after an initial translation from an external dialect. For example, you can apply systematic query rewrites using YAML configurations to customize and optimize your GoogleSQL queries before deploying it.

These features are in Preview.
entry.summary: Translate Impala SQL and GoogleSQL to GoogleSQL with new tools.
entry.title: Feature: SQL translator

entry.content: You can now use custom constraints with an Organization Policy to provide more granular control over specific fields for BigQuery dataset resources. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use custom constraints with Organization Policy for more granular control over BigQuery dataset resources. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Custom Constraints for BigQuery Datasets are Generally Available

entry.content: You can aggregate and deduplicate table data with Gemini assistance in your BigQuery data preparations. These features are generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now aggregate and deduplicate your table data with Gemini assistance in BigQuery data preparations. These features are generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Aggregate and Deduplicate Table Data

entry.content: BigQuery ML now offers the AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES function. Use the AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES function with a TimesFM model to detect anomalies in time series data, using historical data as a baseline. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: The AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES function is now available in BigQuery ML for detecting anomalies in time series data. It uses historical data as a baseline and is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES function

entry.content: BigQuery ML now supports the TimesFM 2.5 time series foundational model. You can use the TimesFM 2.5 model in the AI.FORECAST, AI.EVALUATE, and AI.DETECT_ANOMALIES functions to achieve better forecasting accuracy and lower latency.
entry.summary: BigQuery ML has integrated the TimesFM 2.5 foundational model, enhancing forecasting capabilities.
entry.title: Feature: BigQuery ML Now Supports TimesFM 2.5

entry.content: Partitioning is now available for BigLake tables for Apache Iceberg in BigQuery. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Partitioning is now available for BigLake tables for Apache Iceberg in BigQuery. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Partitioning for BigLake Tables

entry.content: The research paper ARIMA_PLUS: Large-scale, Accurate, Automatic and Interpretable In-Database Time Series Forecasting and Anomaly Detection in Google BigQuery is now publicly available. This paper describes the algorithms behind the ARIMA_PLUS and ARIMA_PLUS_XREG models for time series forecasting and anomaly detection, and demonstrates the high performance, scalability, explainability, and customizability of the models.
entry.summary: A new research paper details ARIMA Plus, a novel system for time series forecasting and anomaly detection within Google BigQuery. The paper highlights its performance, scalability, and interpretability.
entry.title: Announcement: ARIMA Plus in BigQuery

entry.content: You can now generate data insights when you create a DataScan using the Dataplex API. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now generate data insights when you create a DataScan using the Dataplex API. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Data Insights Now Available

entry.content: You can now generate table and column descriptions in all supported Gemini languages when you generate data insights. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now generate table and column descriptions in all supported Gemini languages when generating data insights. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: New Feature: Generate Descriptions in All Supported Gemini Languages

entry.content: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service for Google Ads now supports Google Ads API v21.
entry.summary: The BigQuery Data Transfer Service for Google Ads now supports Google Ads API v21.
entry.title: Announcement: Google Ads API v21 Support

entry.content: You can use the MATCH_RECOGNIZE clause in your SQL queries to filter and aggregate matches across rows in a table. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: The MATCH_RECOGNIZE clause is now generally available for use in SQL queries.
entry.title: Feature: MATCH_RECOGNIZE clause in SQL

entry.content: You can now use custom organization policies with the BigQuery migration service to allow or deny specific operations during a BigQuery migration to meet your organization's compliance and security requirements. This includes an option to disable AI suggestions during a migration. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now use custom organization policies with the BigQuery migration service to allow or deny specific operations during a BigQuery migration. This feature, which is in Preview, helps meet your organization's compliance and security requirements and includes an option to disable AI suggestions during a migration.
entry.title: Feature: Custom organization policies for BigQuery migration now available

entry.content: We have increased the row capacity for pivot tables backed by BigQuery in Connected Sheets from 100,000 to 200,000 rows.
entry.summary: The row capacity for BigQuery-backed pivot tables in Connected Sheets has been increased from 100,000 to 200,000 rows.
entry.title: Feature: Pivot table row capacity doubled for Connected Sheets

entry.content: The Apache Iceberg REST catalog in BigLake metastore is now generally available (GA) with several new features, including BigQuery catalog federation, credential vending, and catalog management in the Google Cloud console.
entry.summary: The Apache Iceberg REST catalog in BigLake metastore has reached general availability. This feature now includes BigQuery catalog federation, credential vending, and catalog management directly within the Google Cloud console.
entry.title: Feature: Apache Iceberg REST catalog in BigLake metastore is now GA

entry.content: You can now group reservations together to prioritize idle slot sharing within the group. Reservations within a reservation group share idle slots with each other before making them available to other reservations in the project, giving you more control over slot allocation for high-priority workloads. This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now group reservations together to prioritize idle slot sharing within the group.
entry.title: Feature: Reservation Groups for Prioritizing Workloads

entry.content: Subscriber email logging lets you log the principal identifiers of users who execute jobs and queries against linked datasets. You can enable logging at the listing level and the data exchange level. The logged data is available in the job_principal_subject field of the INFORMATION_SCHEMA.SHARED_DATASET_USAGE view. This feature is generally available.
entry.summary: New feature: subscriber email logging is now available. This allows you to log the principal identifiers of users who execute jobs and queries against linked datasets. You can enable this feature at the listing or data exchange level.
entry.title: Feature: Subscriber email logging

entry.content:
The BigQuery Data Transfer Service can now transfer data from the following data sources:

  • Facebook Ads
  • Salesforce
  • Salesforce Marketing Cloud
  • ServiceNow

Transfers from these data sources are now generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery Data Transfer Service now supports data transfers from Facebook Ads, Salesforce, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, and ServiceNow. These integrations are now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: New Data Sources Available in BigQuery Data Transfer Service

entry.content: You can now use the Data Engineering Agent to use Gemini in BigQuery to build and modify data pipelines to ingest data into BigQuery. This feature is in preview.
entry.summary: Use the new Data Engineering Agent with Gemini in BigQuery to build and modify data pipelines for ingesting data.
entry.title: Feature: Introducing the Data Engineering Agent

entry.content:
The administrative jobs explorer now includes a job details page to help you diagnose and troubleshoot queries. The Performance tab compiles query information including the execution graph, SQL text, execution history, performance variance, and system load during execution. You can also compare two jobs to identify discrepancies and potential areas to improve query performance.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: A new job details page is now available in the administrative jobs explorer to help diagnose and troubleshoot queries. It includes performance information, execution details, and the ability to compare jobs for performance improvement.
entry.title: Feature: New Job Details Page for Query Troubleshooting

entry.content:
BigQuery now offers the following managed AI functions that use Gemini to help you filter, join, rank, and classify your data:

  • AI.IF: Filter and join text or multimodal data based on a condition described in natural language.
  • AI.SCORE: Rate text or multimodal input to rank your data by quality, similarity, or other criteria.
  • AI.CLASSIFY: Classify text into user-defined categories.

These functions are in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery now offers managed AI functions that leverage Gemini for data manipulation tasks like filtering, joining, ranking, and classification.
entry.title: Feature: Introducing AI Functions in BigQuery

entry.content: You can now use the Apache Arrow format to stream data to BigQuery with the Storage Write API. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: You can now use the Apache Arrow format to stream data to BigQuery with the Storage Write API. This feature is now generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Apache Arrow format support for BigQuery Storage Write API

entry.content: BigQuery is now offering early access to conversational analytics. Conversational analytics accelerates data analysis by enabling quick insights through natural language. Users can chat with their BigQuery data, create custom agents, and access those agents even outside of BigQuery. To enroll in conversational analytics early access, fill out the request form.
entry.summary: BigQuery introduces conversational analytics for faster data insights using natural language. Users can interact with data, build custom agents, and access them outside of BigQuery.
entry.title: Feature: Conversational Analytics in BigQuery

entry.content: You can now use custom constraints with Organization Policy to provide more granular control over specific fields for some BigQuery sharing resources. For more information, see Manage Sharing data exchanges and listings using custom constraints. This feature is in preview.
entry.summary: Organization Policy now supports custom constraints for more granular control over specific fields in BigQuery sharing resources. This feature is currently in preview.
entry.title: Feature: Granular control for BigQuery sharing

entry.content: Support for table parameters in table-value functions (TVFs) has been temporarily disabled. We are working to restore this feature as soon as possible.
entry.summary: Support for table parameters in table-value functions (TVFs) has been temporarily disabled. We are working to restore this feature as soon as possible.
entry.title: Issue: Table parameters in TVFs temporarily disabled

entry.content:
BigQuery ML now offers a built-in TimesFM univariate time series forecasting model that implements Google Research's open source TimesFM model. You can use BigQuery ML's built-in TimesFM model with the following functions:

  • Use AI.FORECAST to perform forecasting. This function now supports a larger context window.
  • Use AI.EVALUATE to evaluate forecasted data against a reference time series based on historical data.

To try using a TimesFM model with the AI.FORECAST function, see Forecast a time series with a TimesFM univariate model.

This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery ML now includes a built-in TimesFM model for univariate time series forecasting. This feature, which is now generally available, allows you to use the AI.FORECAST and AI.EVALUATE functions for more robust forecasting capabilities.
entry.title: Feature: TimesFM Univariate Time Series Forecasting Model

entry.content: BigQuery now supports TransUnion for entity resolution. This feature is generally available (GA).
entry.summary: BigQuery now supports TransUnion for entity resolution. This feature is generally available.
entry.title: Feature: Entity Resolution support in BigQuery

entry.content:
You can now use visualization cells to automatically generate a visualization of any DataFrame in your notebook. You can customize the columns, chart type, aggregations, colors , labels, and title.

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: Generate visualizations from DataFrames in your notebook.
entry.title: Feature: Visualization cells

entry.content:
In BigQuery ML, you can now fully manage open models as Vertex AI endpoints. BigQuery-managed open models offer the following benefits:

  • Manage Vertex AI resource by using SQL queries
  • Automatic or immediate open model undeployment to save costs
  • Customize model deployment machine types or reserve open model resources by using Compute Engine reservations

This feature is in Preview.
entry.summary: BigQuery ML now supports managing open models as Vertex AI endpoints, offering benefits like SQL-based resource management, cost-saving auto-undeployment, and customizable deployment options.
entry.title: Feature: Manage open models in BigQuery ML

entry.content:
The following features are now generally available (GA) in BigQuery Studio:

  • To streamline resource discovery and access, the left Explorer pane has been reorganized into three sections: Explorer, Classic Explorer, and Git repository. You can still use the Classic Explorer, which provides the complete resources tree.
  • In the Explorer pane, you can use the search feature to find BigQuery resources in your organization. The results appear in a new tab in the details pane. You can use filters to narrow your search.
